que: In StateFlow, what is the primary goal?
options: avoid understanding requirements
replace testing entirely
expose observable state holder from ViewModel
skip state/data modeling

que: Which API/concept is most central to StateFlow?
options: manual polling loop for all tasks
random utility class name
StateFlow
legacy XML-only patterns unrelated to this topic

que: What is a recommended best practice for StateFlow?
options: optimize before verifying correctness
mix unrelated responsibilities in one function
ignore edge/error states
collect as state in UI

que: Which is a common mistake when implementing StateFlow?
options: keep boundaries clear
using cold flows for always-on UI state
follow official docs for API usage
write focused tests for behavior

que: For production code, which approach fits StateFlow best?
options: Use StateFlow only as theory with no implementation
Use StateFlow with clear state flow and testable logic
Use StateFlow while bypassing architecture boundaries
Use StateFlow and suppress all warnings

que: A teammate asks where to start with StateFlow. What is best advice?
options: avoid feedback from tests/review
copy an unknown snippet without context
Read official docs (https://kotlinlang.org/api/kotlinx.coroutines/kotlinx-coroutines-core/kotlinx.coroutines.flow/-state-flow/) and implement a small sample
skip running the app after changes

que: Which statement about StateFlow is most accurate?
options: StateFlow is mainly about adding boilerplate
StateFlow removes the need for any testing
StateFlow should be applied identically in every layer
StateFlow is most effective when paired with clear ownership of state/data

que: In a code review for StateFlow, what would you flag?
options: traceable data flow between layers
explicit modeling of UI/data states
small pure functions with clear names
Code that hides failures instead of handling them explicitly

que: Which scenario is the strongest example of using StateFlow correctly?
options: an implementation with no error states
a slide deck with no runnable code
A small, working feature that demonstrates StateFlow in context
a large refactor without tests

que: To learn StateFlow deeply, what should you do next?
options: avoid official references
skip verification to save time
Build, test, profile (if needed), and iterate using StateFlow patterns
memorize definitions only
